question: Will any of these AI companies (Lovable, DeepSeek, or Perplexity) have a funding round in July 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as **Yes** if any of these companies has a funding round listed by TracXn with a date in July 2026: [Lovable](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/lovable/__fO6WUvzcx5q3814ietMd3kzGlRirsatzsTnpxmzLuMI/funding-and-investors),  [DeepSeek](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/deepseek/__1GrZ3pgoi2O-9tMSfF9ka6Sjybc1lgSSZ_qQqo5k2vE/funding-and-investors), or [Perplexity](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/perplexity/__V2BE-5ihMWJ1hNb2_u1W7Gry25JzPFCBg-iNWi94XI8/funding-and-investors).
reference_class: Active late-stage AI unicorns' funding round closing within a specific ~1-week window given an already-public 'in talks' process
reasoning:
Base rate: for a specific ~6-day window, the chance any one of three highly active AI companies logs a new TracXn-dated round is low unless a deal is already imminent. DeepSeek's first-ever $7.4B round closed mid-June 2026 (Tencent/CATL-led) — done before the window opens. Perplexity's most recent raise (~$200M for Comet) was reported June 5-8, 2026, also pre-window, and July coverage frames Perplexity's month as product-focused, not fundraising. Lovable is the live wildcard: in talks since June 5, 2026 for a $12B valuation round (up from $6.6B), but as of the latest available reporting the round remained unclosed, with the company declining to confirm terms. Given active negotiations, some chance it closes in the first days of July, but no evidence yet that it has. Aggregating: low but non-trivial probability, driven almost entirely by uncertainty over whether the Lovable round (or an unreported event) closes by July 6, 21:00 UTC.
[pooled 3 independent runs [0.09, 0.08, 0.07] -> 0.080; the narrative above is the research run's own view (0.09)]
what_would_change_my_mind: A press release or Tracxn listing showing Lovable's $12B round has closed with a July 2026 date
Any news of a surprise new Perplexity or DeepSeek round announced this week
Confirmation that DeepSeek's June round has a secondary July closing/tranche date on Tracxn

missing_evidence: No reporting found between June 8 and July 6, 2026 on Lovable round status, and Tracxn's own funding pages were inaccessible (403) to verify exact listed dates or date-attribution convention (announcement vs. close).
Could not access Tracxn's pages directly (403 blocked) to check exact date attribution conventions or any already-listed July entries

question: Will any of these AI companies (OpenAI, Anthropic, or Mistral AI) have a funding round in July 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as **Yes** if any of these companies has a funding round listed by TracXn with a date in July 2026: [Mistral AI](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/mistral-ai/__SLZq7rzxLYqqA97jtPwO09jLDeb76RVJVb306OhciWU/funding-and-investors#funding-rounds),  [Anthropic](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/anthropic/__SzoxXDMin-NK5tKB7ks8yHr6S9Mz68pjVCzFEcGFZ08/funding-and-investors#funding-rounds), or [OpenAI](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/openai/__kElhSG7uVGeFk1i71Co9-nwFtmtyMVT7f-YHMn4TFBg/funding-and-investors).
reference_class: Frontier AI lab (OpenAI/Anthropic/Mistral) mega-funding-round cadence in the 2026 AI funding boom
reasoning:
Anthropic just closed Series H (Tracxn-dated Apr 21, 2026) and is now on an IPO track (confidential S-1 filed June 1, 2026), explicitly framed as its last private round — new dated entry in July is unlikely (~10%). OpenAI's Series F round has shown a pattern of new dated tranches on Tracxn as fresh investors join (Feb 27, Mar 20, Apr 22, 2026) but nothing since — a ~10-week gap as of now; SoftBank's already-committed $10B tranche executed July 1 is unlikely to appear as a new distinct entry since anchor investors were dated at initial close (~20%). Mistral's rumored €3B/€20B round was only in 'early discussions' as of June 12, 2026 with no confirmed terms or close date — plausible to close in July given competitive urgency but more likely slips later (~22%). Combining three roughly independent, individually sub-50% chances yields an aggregate around 40%.
[pooled 3 independent runs [0.4, 0.4, 0.35] -> 0.383; the narrative above is the research run's own view (0.40)]
what_would_change_my_mind: Confirmation Mistral's €3B round has signed definitive terms/closed
News of a new OpenAI investor tranche or Series G announcement
Any report Anthropic is doing a private round despite IPO filing
Direct Tracxn page update showing a July 2026 dated entry for any of the three

missing_evidence: Could not directly load Tracxn's live funding-and-investors pages (blocked, 403) to check for any new dated entries since the Apr 2026 snapshot; Tracxn's exact methodology for whether follow-on tranches to existing investors generate new discrete round entries (vs. only updating an existing round's

No direct evidence on Tracxn's internal methodology for whether follow-on SoftBank tranches of an already-recorded OpenAI commitment get separate dated entries; no confirmed timeline for Mistral round closing.

question: What will be the result of the 2026 Florida Republican gubernatorial primary?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as the result of the [2026 Republican primary](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Florida_gubernatorial_election#Republican_primary) in the Florida gubernatorial election, according to official results or [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions).
options: Donalds wins, with ≥75% of the vote
Donalds wins, with >65% and <75% of the vote
Donalds wins, with >55% and ≤65% of the vote
Donalds wins, with ≤55% of the vote
reasoning: Byron Donalds has near-total institutional consolidation (Trump, Rick Scott, House leadership) against a fractured, weak field (Collins, Fishback, Renner combined ~15% decided support). Polling rose from 46% (Mar) to 54% (Jun) with 31-39% undecided; late-breaking undecideds in blowout primaries typically favor the recognized frontrunner disproportionately, pointing to a final vote share likely in the mid-60s to low-70s. Polymarket prices Donalds winning outright at ~95-96%, consistent with a very low probability of an outright loss. Five internal draws across trend-extrapolation, historical-comparison, weak-opposition, downside-risk, and market-anchored framings centered on 63-72% vote share, with the 65-75% bucket modal.

question: How many EU member states will the AI Act National Implementation tracker classify as having clear market surveillance and notifying authorities on August 31, 2026?
resolution_criterion: This question resolves as the number of National Competent Authorities classified as "Clear" at the EU's  [Overview of all AI Act National Implementation Plans](https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/national-implementation-plans/) tracker, when checked by Metaculus on or after August 31, 2026.
reasoning: Tracker (artificialintelligenceact.eu) shows 9/27 states 'Clear' as of last update 17 June 2026, essentially unchanged from ~March 2026 (8-9), indicating a multi-month plateau. Statutory deadline was Aug 2025; compliance has been chronically slow (EU cross-border regulatory harmonization historically drags for years, cf. GDPR SAs). France dropped its designation provisions from pending legislation. August is EU summer-recess season, reducing odds of a burst of national legislative action before Aug 31. Modest upward drift (to 10-11) is plausible as 1-2 'partial clarity' states finalize, but a jump to 12+ within under 8 weeks would require an unprecedented acceleration versus the observed flat trend.
